scene: A tiny stick kid lies back in tall green grass under a huge slow yellow sun, the word SUMMER hand-lettered very wide across the top, composed tall to fill the vertical frame. Lazy and endless. Selective pop: the big yellow sun.

An adult stick figure watches a wall calendar's pages blast off and fly up and away in a fast blur, big hand-lettered DECEMBER, composed tall. A year gone in a blink. Selective pop: the red motion lines.

A big bold calendar with 365 hand-lettered large, and a wall clock spinning fast beside it with red speed lines and a big red question mark. Why faster? Composed tall. Selective pop: the red speed lines.

Two pie charts stacked tall: the top one has one huge slice labeled AGE 5, the bottom one a razor-thin sliver labeled AGE 50. The obvious answer. Composed tall. Selective pop: the big slice and the thin red sliver.
A neat pie chart with a big red X slammed across it and hand-lettered MOSTLY WRONG, composed tall, mostly empty. Selective pop: the red X.
A doodle brain glowing with a red arrow pointing to it hand-lettered MEMORY, and a small wall clock with a red X beside it. The real reason. Composed tall. Selective pop: the brain and the red arrow.

A tall split frame: on top a kid surrounded by bright glowing 1ST polaroids, packed and colorful; on the bottom an adult beside a dull stack of identical gray boxes labeled SAME. Firsts versus routine. Selective pop: the glowing polaroids against the flat gray.
A floppy-disk SAVE icon with a big bold red X over it and an unsaved file fading away, composed tall, mostly empty. You stopped saving the file. Selective pop: the red X.
